_comment: Signals the ranking prompt (prompts/score-rank.md) uses to compute each event cluster's importance. These are guidance weights for the LLM, not a rigid formula — but the hard rules below are absolute.

hardRules._coOccurrenceNote: If an event involves BOTH persons, or both appear together in the same article (same URL seen under both persons' feeds → item.coOccurrence === true), that cluster is FORCED to the top, above everything else, regardless of its other scores.

coverageBreadth.desc: How many distinct outlets covered the same event. The strongest 'importance' signal.

sourceTier.desc: Credibility/reach of the outlets. Le Monde / Reuters / AFP / Politico > regional > tabloid/aggregator.

centrality.desc: Is the person the protagonist of the event, or only mentioned in passing? Protagonist scores high; passing mention scores low. (This is the '占比' signal.)

velocity.desc: Breaking / fast-developing today scores higher than a slow ongoing topic.

personalRelevanceAxis._note: A SEPARATE axis from public importance. Flag items that matter a lot to the person's own trajectory even if media coverage is thin. Surface these even if they wouldn't make top-3 on public importance alone.
attal: anything tied to the 2027 presidential bid
sejourne: EU industrial strategy / Made in Europe
his standing within von der Leyen's Commission
